summary: Excellent soundboard recording of a mellow 1980 show highlighted by a scorching Scarlet > Fire sequence and tight second‑set flow.
review: Fans rave about the crystal‑clear SBD source, noting crisp separation and an A+‑level mix. Musically the set shines with a laid‑back first half and a second‑set that bursts into a hot Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain, stellar China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider, Ramble On Rose, and a soaring Sugar Magnolia, while Jerry’s guitar work and Brent’s subtle keyboards add depth. A few reviewers cite uneven vocals and an abrupt Not Fade Away, but overall the performance is regarded as one of the stronger early‑80s outings.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is ~4.4/5; recording quality is described as excellent and show quality receives consistent praise for several standout songs, outweighing a few minor criticisms.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the sound "crisp," "A+," and highlight superb channel separation; no major dropouts reported, only occasional comments about uneven vocals.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is relaxed and well‑played; the second set kicks into high gear with seamless transitions (Scarlet > Fire, CC Rider, Ramble On Rose) that keep the momentum flowing and showcase strong improvisational jams.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Nice guitar work throughout, especially on Black Peter; some reviewers note his tone is solid but not always as exciting as other eras.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass presence is felt throughout the mellow vibe of the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland adds understated yet plentiful keyboard texture; described as “delirious” during peaks.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy and Mickey are “smokin’” during transitions, providing a driving rhythmic foundation.
